title: Schefflera calva
intro: Schefflera calva, also known as the dwarf umbrella tree, is a small tree native to the tropical regions of South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against certain types of cancer. The bioactive constituents of Schefflera calva include alkaloids, flavonoids, and lignans, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Schefflera calva is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Schefflera calva is classified within the family Araliaceae. This family includes various other species of plants commonly known as ivy arums or arums. Schefflera calva is a part of the genus Schefflera, which consists of multiple species of flowering plants. The genus Schefflera is further grouped within the order Apiales. This classification places Schefflera calva within the broader group of flowering plants, known as angiosperms.
